skill: Linear inequalities in one or two variables
stem: A particular biologist classifies an individual of a certain species of fish as heavy if its typical mass when fully grown is more than 50 kilograms. Each of the following inequalities represents the possible masses m, in kilograms, for a specific fish species when fully grown. Which inequality represents the possible masses m, in kilograms, for a heavy fish species?
rationale:
Choice C is correct. A heavy fish has mass > 50 kg, so every value in the range must exceed 50. Only 62 < m < 85 satisfies this for all values.

Choices A, B, and D include masses at or below 50 kg, so they do not all represent heavy fish.
